name: The crystal is a compression of category theory

phase_0.domain_name: The crystal is a compression of category theory
phase_0.boundary_condition: The 12-primitive basis — the complete set of IMASM opcodes that form the terminal anchor for all categorical operations, containing the entire system of 15 operations and their 17.28M equivalence classes.

VINIT.justification: The bare abstract category before any self-modeling, criticality, or winding. It is the uninitialized void from which all crystal types emerge — objects, morphisms, and composition without the 12-primitive structure.

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary that contains the whole system. Every operation in CC projects onto this basis; it is the terminal anchor that seals the crystal, defining the equivalence classes and the mixed-radix encoding.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism from CC to Crystal — the projection onto the 12-primitive basis. Maps any object to its 12-tuple address, moving toward the compressed representation.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism from Crystal back to CC — the inverse of the projection, recovering the equivalence class from the 12-tuple address. Descent from the compressed form to the original category.

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of the 15 operations (L1–L6, I1–I4, A1–A5) within CC. Morphisms compose to form new morphisms; the crystal inherits this composition through the quotient map.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference — an object in CC recognizes itself through its representable presheaf. The Yoneda embedding is the identity morphism of the category, the element that is itself, and it survives the quotient as a self-referential 12-tuple.

FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: a single operation in CC branches into two distinct 12-tuple outcomes. Folding operations change the 12-tuple, moving between crystal types — e.g., a limit computation splits into its true (convergent) and false (divergent) branches.

FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches reconstitute the original operation. Unfolding operations are intra-fiber — they are invisible to the 12-primitive projection. Fusing the true and false branches of a limit recovers the original limit object exactly.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the limit exists, the colimit is universal, the adjunction is satisfied. The true arm of the FSPLIT anchored by successful categorical construction.

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: the limit does not exist, the colimit fails, the adjunction is not satisfied. The false arm of the FSPLIT anchored by failed categorical construction.

ENGAGR.justification: A paradice held without resolution: an operation that is both folding and unfolding simultaneously. For example, a dagger structure that is both a forward morphism and its reverse, held in the 12-tuple as a single 11-state without collapsing to either true or false.

IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: the permanent, append-only record of the 12-tuple address. Once crystal_encode writes the 27 × 1024 × 625 encoding, it is irreversible — a Lean-verified bijection that cannot be undone.

phase_2.split_input: a single categorical operation (e.g., a limit computation) in CC
phase_2.fuse_result: the original categorical operation (the limit computation itself, with its internal CC structure intact)

phase_3.void_description: prima_materia (CC) before any 12-primitive projection — the bare abstract category without self-modeling, criticality, or winding. Objects exist but have no crystal address.
phase_3.true_description: A 12-tuple that encodes a successful categorical construction — limit exists, colimit is universal, adjunction holds. The crystal address is in the true register.
phase_3.false_description: A 12-tuple that encodes a failed categorical construction — limit diverges, colimit is not universal, adjunction fails. The crystal address is in the false register.
phase_3.both_description: A 12-tuple that encodes a paradice — a dagger structure that is simultaneously a morphism and its reverse, a bicameral compromise held in the 11-state without resolution. Both true and false registers are active.

steps.domain_action: prima_materia (CC) — the bare abstract category exists as uninitialized void, before any 12-primitive structure is imposed.
crystal_encode — project an object from CC onto the 12-primitive basis, moving toward the compressed crystal representation.
folding — the projection branches into two 12-tuple outcomes: one where the categorical construction succeeds (true arm), one where it fails (false arm).
convergent branch — the true arm is anchored: the limit exists, the colimit is universal, the adjunction is satisfied.
divergent branch — the false arm is anchored: the limit does not exist, the colimit fails, the adjunction is not satisfied.
composition — chain the successful true-arm operations (L1–L6, I1–I4, A1–A5) to build the full 12-tuple.
composition — chain the failed false-arm operations to build the alternative 12-tuple.
dagger structure — a paradice: the operation is both a forward morphism and its reverse, held in the 11-state without resolution. Both true and false 12-tuples are active simultaneously.
unfolding — the true and false branches reconstitute the original categorical operation. The intra-fiber richness of CC is recovered exactly, invisible to the 12-primitive projection.
Yoneda embedding — the object recognizes itself through its representable presheaf. The identity morphism survives the quotient as a self-referential 12-tuple.
crystal_decode — reverse the projection, recovering the equivalence class from the 12-tuple address back into CC.
mixed-radix encoding — permanently record the 27 × 1024 × 625 address. The Lean-verified bijection crystal_decode ∘ crystal_encode = id is fixed, append-only, irreversible.
12-primitive basis — the closed boundary seals the crystal. All 15 operations are contained within the 12-primitive terminal anchor, completing the compression.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The quotient map CC → Crystal — projects every object and morphism in CC onto its 12-tuple equivalence class, translating domain intentions into crystal operations.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The 12-primitive basis itself — components communicate by sharing 12-tuple addresses; two objects are equivalent iff they share the same 12-tuple, enabling inter-component communication through the mixed-radix encoding.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The 17.28M address space — each 12-tuple is stored as a 27 × 1024 × 625 mixed-radix number, retrievable via crystal_decode.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The 4-stage derivation (as_above_induction) — determines the order of the 15 operations: logical (L1–L6), then inductive (I1–I4), then algebraic (A1–A5), crystallizing all 12 primitives in sequence.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Lean-verified bijection crystal_decode ∘ crystal_encode = id — the canonical reference store that guarantees the round-trip identity, serving as the bootstrap for all crystal operations.

phase_6.cycle_cost: The 15 operations in CC — each operation consumes categorical structure (objects, morphisms, composition) to produce a 12-tuple.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: prima_materia (CC) with its full 15-operation structure — objects, morphisms, composition, limits, colimits, adjunctions, dagger, Yoneda, etc.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The crystal with its 12-primitive basis and 17.28M addresses — the same 15 operations compressed into 12-tuples, with the quotient map preserving all equivalence classes.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the crystal_decode ∘ crystal_encode = id bijection guarantees that the round-trip recovers the original categorical structure exactly; no information is lost, only compressed into the 12-primitive basis.

  .withGram Gamma_seq <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow D_wedge D_wedge R_lr)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := D_wedge | initial object — ground of distinction (prima_materia (CC) — the bare abstract category exists as uninitialized void,...)

  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := G_beth) (folding — the projection branches into two 12-tuple outcomes: one where the categorical construction succeeds (true arm), one where it fails (false arm).) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := one_one)

      .seq
        (.arrow F_ell G_beth one_one)  -- [6] CLINK | fid := F_ell | composition — regime coherence (composition — chain the failed false-arm operations to build the alternative ...)
        (.arrow n_m G_beth one_one)  -- [7] ENGAGR | stoi := n_m | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (dagger structure — a paradice: the operation is both a forward morphism and i...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow one_one one_one Gamma_seq)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := one_one (unfolding — the true and false branches reconstitute the original categorical operation. The intra-fiber richness of CC is recovered exactly, invisible to the 12-primitive projection.)
  (.arrow Gamma_seq one_one P_asym)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := Gamma_seq | identity — self-imscription (Yoneda embedding — the object recognizes itself through its representable pre...)
  (.arrow P_asym Gamma_seq Omega_Z)  -- [10] AREV | pol := P_asym | reverse morphism — parity flip (crystal_decode — reverse the projection, recovering the equivalence class fro...)
  (.arrow Omega_Z P_asym T_network)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := Omega_Z | irreversible fixation — winding number (mixed-radix encoding — permanently record the 27 × 1024 × 625 address. The Le...)
  (.arrow T_network Omega_Z D_wedge)  -- [12] TANCH | top := T_network | terminal object — connectivity boundary (12-primitive basis — the closed boundary seals the crystal. All 15 operations...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def the_crystal_is_a_compression_of_category_theory_true_arm : IGProtocol D_wedge T_network :=
  (the_crystal_is_a_compression_of_category_theory_protocol).restrictToEVALT
